skill.name: migration-tracker
skill.description: Tracks Portfolio Buddy migration status, feature parity, known issues, and technical debt for development work.
skill.summary: Track Portfolio Buddy migration progress and feature parity.
skill.author: 5MinFutures

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many Ruby backtick and weak cryptography patterns, but review found Markdown formatting and project documentation rather than executable Ruby code or cryptographic implementation. The only confirmed risk is documented use of visible external commands such as git and npm, which can change a repository when a user chooses to run them.

low_findings.title: Repository-changing command guidance
low_findings.description: The skill documents git and npm commands for recovery and cleanup work. These commands are visible and relevant, but they can modify dependencies or repository state if executed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and easy to locate. They appear to support legitimate migration work, not hidden execution or malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static command findings are mostly Markdown false positives
low_findings.description: Most Ruby backtick detections are inline Markdown around identifiers, filenames, and function names. No evidence found of Ruby code execution or hidden shell invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are documentation text using Markdown backticks. They do not define scripts, execution hooks, or interpolated shell commands.

low_findings.title: Static weak cryptography findings are false positives
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography detections match migration text such as metrics, schemas, and documentation labels. No evidence found of hash algorithms, encryption code, or credential handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a Markdown migration tracker and does not contain cryptographic code. The flagged text is descriptive project documentation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Visible external commands in documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes command examples for git recovery and package removal. These are not automatically executed, but users should review repository state before running them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are present in fenced or inline documentation. The risk is operational because execution would be user-directed and visible.

content.value_statement: Migration work becomes risky when feature status and known issues are scattered. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concise context for Portfolio Buddy parity work.
actual_capabilities: Summarizes the status of 40 Portfolio Buddy migration features.
Documents completed database integration and dual CSV database support.
Lists known issues, technical debt, and migration priorities.
Records commit references for recently completed migration work.
Compares old and new app features for parity planning.
Explains current stack choices such as React, TypeScript, Vite, and Supabase.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not run migration tasks automatically.
Referenced source files are outside the skill package and must be checked separately.
Some status details can become stale after new commits land.
It is specific to Portfolio Buddy and is not a generic migration framework.

use_cases.title: Plan Feature Parity Work
use_cases.description: Identify completed, in-progress, and missing features before starting migration tasks.

use_cases.title: Understand Legacy Decisions
use_cases.description: Review why Supabase, Chart.js, React hooks, and TypeScript were selected during migration.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Technical Debt
use_cases.description: Use documented issues and ICE scores to choose cleanup work with clear impact.

prompt_templates.title: Check Migration Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the migration tracker to summarize current Portfolio Buddy migration status and the top unfinished features.

prompt_templates.title: Find Parity Gaps
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare old and new Portfolio Buddy feature parity, then list the highest priority regressions to address next.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Bug Fix
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the migration tracker to explain the relevant known issues and files before I fix a database loading bug.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Refactoring Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the migration tracker to create a refactoring plan for the largest components, including risks and verification steps.

output_examples.input: What migration work should I do next?
output_examples.output: A prioritized list covering error boundaries, Excel export, Recharts removal, TypeScript cleanup, and testing setup.

output_examples.input: Why does the app support both CSV and database data?
output_examples.output: A short explanation of backward compatibility, Supabase strategy loading, and format auto-detection for trade rows.

output_examples.input: What features are still missing from the old app?
output_examples.output: A parity summary showing Excel export, PDF export, symbol filtering, and historical comparison gaps.

best_practices: Verify the latest repository state before relying on documented commit status.
Use the tracker before changing migration-related metrics, data loading, or parity features.
Confirm referenced file paths and line numbers in the current codebase before editing.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the tracker as a source of live production data.
Do not run documented git or npm commands without reviewing repository state.
Do not assume old skill claims are accurate when the tracker says they were corrected.

faq.question: What project is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is for the Portfolio Buddy 2 migration from an older Portfolio Buddy app.

faq.question: Does this skill execute migration scripts?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation and context only.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What are the main unfinished features?
faq.answer: Advanced filtering, Excel and PDF export, historical comparison UI, multi-period analysis, and risk scenario modeling remain incomplete.

faq.question: Why is the security risk not safe?
faq.answer: The skill includes visible git and npm command examples that can change a repository if executed.

faq.question: How should developers use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it to understand migration history, parity gaps, known issues, and technical debt before planning code changes.
